title: The One With the Holiday Armadillo

lines.text: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Chandler is on the phone, Rachel and Monica are sitting in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Buh-bye. (Hangs up the phone) I just got us reservations at Michelle's and tickets to the Musicman to celebrate our first holiday season as a betroughed couple.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Betrothed... (Corrects him)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...betrothed couple.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering carrying a skull) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel and Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Haaaa... (Puts the skull on the table) ... ahhhh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Skull?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, it's my mom's.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (freaking out) Oh my god!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, no. It's not! It's not my mom. It belonged to mom. Yeah, no, she used to put it out every Christmas to remind us, that even though it's Christmas, people still die. And, you can put candy in it. (She grabs the skull, pulls out a stick of licorice, and takes a bite.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Offering Ross the skull) Licorice?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Thinking it over) Sure! (Takes one) Hey, I just found out, I get Ben for the holidays this year.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Ohh! That's great!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you gonna dress up as Santa?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nope. I mean, I know Susan does every year, but I think I wanna take this year to teach him all about Hanukkah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And maybe I could teach Ben about the Christmas skull and how people die.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You may need to use this year to teach Ben about Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmm.
Joey comes out of the bathroom reading a newspaper

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. (He exits)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Monica) Did you know he was in there?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How long have we been home?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: About a half an hour.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Lovely!
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Chandler is getting a cup of coffee and sits down next to Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you know what I was thinking? When we get married, are you gonna change your last name to Bing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bing's weird.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Oh, hey, you guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey. Guess what! My landlord just called and my apartment is gonna get ready soon, so I guess I'll be moving out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ahh, Phoebe, I'm gonna miss you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, you will be very sad. All right, well I gotta go tell Rachel the good news.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh! You guys gonna be living together again?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, she's just so much fun with Joey, I just assumed, she'd still be living with him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why do you think, she's having so much fun living with Joey?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No reason, except...she...told...me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really? So she said, she didn't wanna live with me anymore?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No, she didn't say that. I-I-I think you should talk to Monica now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, don't worry about it. I'm sure she wants to live with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're sure? You're absolutely sure?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, no. But, um, I bet she probably does.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Probably? Yeah, I don't like that word. (Chandler and Monica look surprised) Kind of what 'probably' really means. Yeah, uh-huh. Yeah, oh, "Your mom probably won't kill herself," y'know? I'm sorry, but I'm not hanging all my hopes of Rachel and I living together on-on "Probably!" Y'know? You gotta take care of yourselves! (She starts to walk out) In this world history teaches us nothing! (Exits)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bing doesn't seem so weird now, does it?
Joey and Rachel's, Joey is sitting behind a red drum set.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Great, you're home! Guess what Phoebe got me for Christmas! (Starts drumming.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Drums?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (yelling) No! Drums!
Michelle's, Chandler and Monica enter.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to the Maitre d') Hi, could we get two burritos to go, please? (Laughs.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry. But not that sorry, 'cause you don't have to live with it. Um, we have a reservation under the name Chandler Bing.

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: Oh-kay, we'll have a table for you in about 45 minutes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Forty-five minutes? We have tickets to the Musicman at 8:00.

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: I'm sorry. Christmas is a very busy time, sir.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is this because of the burrito thing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (pulling Chandler away from the Maitre d') You need to give him money.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Give him money? It was a joke!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, to get a table! Places like are always shakin' you down. Everybody wants to be paid off.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right, calm down, O'Mally. I'll slip him some money.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You've got to be smooth about it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, I can be smooth. (Walks back to the Maitre d', very smoothly) Listen, we're a little bit in a hurry, so, if you can get us a table a little quicker, I'd appreciate it. (Shakes his hand)

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: Of course, sir.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (Walks back to Monica)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How did it go?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Had the money in the wrong hand. (Shows her his left hand with the money in it)
Ross's apartment, Ross is whirling Ben around.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wooooooo, hehehe. Hey, ahh, you don't feel like you're gonna throw up, do ya?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I do, so let's... (Ben and Ross sit down on the couch) So, Ben, you uh, you know what holiday is coming up, don't ya?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Christmas.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yep, and you know what other holiday is coming up?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Christmas eve.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, but also (Pauses to let Ben answer, but he doesn't.) Hanukkah! See, you're part Jewish, and-and Hanukkah is a Jewish holiday.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Santa has reindeers that can fly!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right, um, but, on Hanukkah, Hanukkah is a celebration of a miracle. See, years and years ago there were these people called the Maccabees.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (singing) Jingle bells, jingle bells...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (interrupts him) Okay, that's right, yes, but on Hanukkah, uh, we sing, uh (Sings) Dreidel, dreidel, dreidel, I made you out of clay.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (singing) Rudolph, the red-nosed reindeer...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (interrupts him again) Okay, it's not a contest.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: When is Santa coming?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, how about this year, instead of Santa, we have fun celebrating Hanukkah?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: No Santa? Was I bad?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! Oh, no-no-no. Hey, you weren't bad, you've been very good, Ben.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Santa's mad at me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, hey-hey, come on, (He grabs Ben and sits him on his lap) Ben, Santa is not mad at you, okay? Hey, you're-you're his favorite little guy!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: So Santa's coming?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (disappointed) Yes! Santa's coming!
Michelle's, Chandler and Monica are discussing how to bribe the Maitre d'.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's easy! Just keep it casual! Give him a kind word, shake his hand and give him the money!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How do you know so much about this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Richard used to do it, didn't he?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We'd be eating our soup right now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mustached bastard...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sees two people exit) Okay, those people just left, come on! Quick! Give him the money and get their table!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (walks up to the Maitre d') Excuse me...
[Chandler can't find his money in the pocket. In the meantime, another couple shows up, and Chandler turns away to look for his money]

lines.speaker: Male Guest
lines.text: (to the Maitre d') Good evening. (Shakes his hand)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (finds his money) Ahh-hahaha! (Turns around to give the Maitre d' his money, but he isn't there anymore)
The Hallway, Phoebe comes up the stairs and hears drumming coming from Joey and Rachel's, so she enters smiling and then sees that Rachel, not Joey, is the one playing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (stopping at Phoebe's entrance) Ha!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So you like the drums! That's, that's great! Y'know, I was worried, that, you know, they would maybe an unbearable living situation. All right, okay, well, apparently not! So, yay!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey, Pheebs, check it out, we already learned a song. (To Rachel) Ready? One, two, three, four...
[Rachel hits some tom-toms and ends up on the 'crash'-cymbal, which is in fact a ride-cymbal, but whatever...]

lines.speaker: Rachel and Joey
lines.text: Tequila!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's fun. (She exits disappointedly.)
Commercial Break
Central Perk, Gunther is serving Chandler and Monica coffee.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you Gunther, put it there. (He gets up, and shakes Gunther's hand. A bunch of coins fall out his hand. He sits down next to Monica.) Definitely not easier with coins. (Joey gets up and picks up the coins. Chandler thinks, Joey is just helping him to pick them up.) Thank you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you. (He gets up and puts on his jacket.) (Phoebe enters) Hey Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here. (Gives her the coins) Now I only owe you $49.50.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! (she sits down next to him)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If you wanna give Joey a Christmas present that disrupts the entire building, why not get him something a little bit more subtle, like a wrecking ball, or a vile of small pox to release in the hallway?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's not just the drum noise. Every five minutes, Joey throws his sticks in the air, and I have to hear, "Oh my eye! Oh god, my eye!" I mean, it is so annoying.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, thank you. You see, this is how normal people are supposed to react to drums.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, you got Joey drums to annoy Rachel, so she wouldn't wanna live there anymore?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe on some level.
Joey and Rachel's, Joey is sitting behind the drums wearing safety goggles, hitting them with his sticks as Rachel watches.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, y'know that you could just not throw the sticks up in the air.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What is Rock 'n' Roll about that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering with an aquarium covered by a towel) Hey, Joey, I got you another present. (She puts it on the counter)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh wait, before you tell me what it is! (He plays a drum-roll) Okay, what is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's a...tarantula! (Joey almost falls down from his drum-stool jumping up) Oh! God! Rachel, look, I'm sorry. What was I thinking giving Joey this big, gross, scary spider in such a poorly constructed cage?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you talking about? I love them! (Looks into the cage) Yeah, I had a tarantula when I was a kid. But it-it died, because my cat ate it. And then, then my cat died. But Joey, isn't this cool?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is it on me? I feel, I feel like it's on me! I got, hey! (He storms into his room)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, isn't that adorable? Joey is afraid of the tarantula.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sarcastic) Ah, yeah, he's so adorable, God, he's just so much fun, Joey is the best, I'm glad you're having so much fun here. (She turns around, about to leave)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Wait-wait a minute, what? Phoebe, what's the matter?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Our apartment is ready.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And that makes you angry because...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because you would rather live here with Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Where did you get that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica and Chandler said that you were having so much fun here. And apparently no amount of drums or tarantulas is gonna change that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hm?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did you get all this stuff for Joey to try and drive me out of the apartment? Honey, if you wanted to do that, you might as well just gotten him a fish, you know how fish freaked me out!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (nods along) Fish!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It wouldn't have mattered anyway, Phoebe, you and I are, are gonna live together, we're roommates; that's the deal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, but I wanted you to want to live with me, but okay, if you're having so much fun over here...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it's so much more fun with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We did have fun, didn't we?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We did!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, anyway, they say, if we want, we can see it tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I would love to!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay, okay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good, good, good, good, good. (She runs to the drums and gets the sticks)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great, all right, okay, and Monica ask me to make the drumming stop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (with the sticks in her hands) Um... (She goes to the tarantula-cage and puts the sticks into it) Done!
Halloween Adventure, a costume shop, there is a salesman behind the counter, Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Salesman
lines.text: Hello, Sir. You're here to return those pants?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, these are my pants.

lines.speaker: Salesman
lines.text: Oh. Okay! How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, uh, do you have a Santa-outfit left?

lines.speaker: Salesman
lines.text: Two days before Christmas? Sorry, man.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay look, do-do, you have anything Christmassy? I promised my son, and I really don't want to disappoint him, um, come on, I...uh, you gotta have something.
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica has just opened the door for Ross who is costumed as an Armadillo. Ben is standing next to her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm the holiday armadillo! I'm a friend of Santa's and he sent me here to wish you (Points to Ben) a Merry Christmas!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What happened to Santa, Holiday Armadillo?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Monica) Santa was unavailable so close to Christmas.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow, come in, have a seat. You must be exhausted coming all the way from...Texas.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Texas?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right, Ben. I'm Santa's representative for all the southern states. And Mexico! But, Santa sent me here to give you these presents, Ben. (He tries to bend down to pick up the bag with the presents, but can't because of the costume) Maybe the Lady will help me with these presents.
[Monica picks up the bag, while Ross closes the door and hits Monica with his tail. They walk into the living room, and Monica empties the bag.]

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Wow! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're welcome, Ben. Merry Christmas, ooh, and Happy Hanukkah!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Are you for Hanukkah, too? Because I'm part-Jewish.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (gasps) You are? Me, too!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because Armadillos also wandered in the desert?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Monica) You wanna wander in the hall? (to Ben) Ooh, hey Ben, what if the Holiday Armadillo told you all about the festival of lights?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Cool!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on Ben.
[Monica and Ben sit down on the couch.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Years and years ago there were these people called the Maccabees...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering in a Santa costume) Ho, ho, ho! Merry Christmas!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Santa! (Runs to Chandler and hugs him)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! (Grunts as Ben hits him at full speed.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you doing here, Santa?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'm here to see my old buddy Ben. What are you doing here, weird...turtle-man?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm the Holiday Armadillo, your part-Jewish friend. You sent me here to give Ben some presents. Remember?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Did you bring me any presents, Santa?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You bet I did, Ben, put it there! (He shakes Ben's hand, but the money falls out of his hands) (to Monica) Well, it would have worked this time, if his hands weren't so damn small! (Realizes, that Ben is standing right there) Ho, ho, ho!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay Ben, why don't you come open some more presents, and Santa, the Armadillo, and I have a little talk in the kitchen? There's a sentence, I never thought I'd say.
[They walk to the kitchen; everyone is lowering their voices]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Chandler) What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You called everyone and said you were having trouble finding a Santa costume, so I borrowed one from a guy at work!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you, but, but you gotta leave.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Because, I'm finally getting him excited about Hanukkah, and, and you're-you're wrecking it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But I didn't get to shape my belly like a bowl full of jelly.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry, Chandler but this, this is really important to me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine, I'll give the suit back.
[Ross turns around and walks back to Ben.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler) Hey, you think, you can keep it another night? (She has a really teasing look on her face and keeps twirling Chandler's beard.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Santa? Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, is that okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did your Dad ever dress up like Santa?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Then it's okay! (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay Ben, Santa has to go. Say good-bye!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: No! Why does he have to go?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because, if Santa and the Holiday...Armadillo? (Ross nods) ...are ever in the same room for too long the universe will implode. Merry Christmas!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: No! Why can't the Armadillo leave? I want Santa!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine, I-I give up. Santa, Santa can stay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'll stay, but only because I wanna hear about Hanukkah. Ben, will you sit here with Santa and learn about Hanukkah?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Okay, Santa!
Ross mouths to Chandler, "Thank you," and he mouths, "You're welcome," back.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, it's time for the story of Hanukkah. Years and years ago, there were these people called the Maccabees.
[Joey enters in a Superman-costume]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering wearing a Superman costume) Merry Christmas!
Phoebe and Rachel's, they are entering to check out the newly refurbished apartment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow! Look at this place!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this is terrible. Oh, they've made so many changes I can't even feel my grandmother's presence anymore-Ooh! New sconces!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (yelling from another room) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (returning) Okay, remember uh, remember how you told me that your grandmother put up that wall to make that into two bedrooms?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And remember how you always said you were afraid the landlord would find out and then tear it down?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you really not know where I'm going with this? (Phoebe nods, "No.") It left! It's one huge room!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no! (She runs to see.) (Running back, excitedly) Oh! Wow!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: See?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I guess we'll just have to put the wall back up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You can't, because of the new skylight!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There's a skylight?! (Runs to see and yells from the bedroom.) Wow!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So what should we do? Should we start looking for a new place?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (returning slowly) Y'know I'm-I'm sensing that um, my grandmother would not be comfortable with that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah? Startin' to feel her again there are we?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A little bit, yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs is your grandmother maybe saying that you should live here alone?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You heard her too?! You have the gift!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, it's okay. I like living with Joey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh please, I hate packing, it's closer to work, and we do have fun. Although, I'm really gonna miss living with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh me too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know. (They hug.) Oh-oh, wait did you hear that-hear that? Listen, I'm gettin' something from your grandmother, she said that since you get to keep the one bedroom apartment you should give Rachel the purple chair?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I do not hear that.
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Santa (Chandler), Superman (Joey), Ben, and Monica are listening to the Holiday Armadillo (Ross) finish telling the story of Hanukkah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...and the miracle was that that little bit of oil that should've just lasted just one day, burned for...

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (answering him) Eight whole days.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right, and that's why we celebrate Hanukkah today. The end.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Awesome!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: My favorite part was when Superman flew all the Jews out of Egypt. (Glaring at Joey who's nodding.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The Armadillo was actually not so thrilled about that part! Okay Ben, it's time to light the Hanukkah candles! (Santa, Superman, Monica, Ben, and the Holiday Armadillo go over to the menorah to light the candles.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering with Phoebe) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (seeing the collection of characters.) Wow! It looks like the Easter Bunny's funeral in here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, come on, we're-we're-we're lighting the candles!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.
They both go over to light the candles.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I understand why Superman is here, but why is there a porcupine at the Easter Bunny's funeral?
Ending Credits
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is closing the door on the tarantula cage.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I got it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (yelling from the bedroom) Is it back in the cage?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Its back in cage!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cage closed?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, would you just come out here and stop being such a baby!
Joey throws open his door and stands there still in the Superman costume then slowly makes his way to the bathroom while keeping both eyes on the cage.
End
